reference_policy: reference[0] is a secondary difficulty signal, not benchmark gold
scorer_semantics: Diagnostic candidate coverage only; scorer semantics are unchanged. Purist/Pragmatic equivalence uses gan_frequency_deterministic_v1 category functions for coverage stratification.

fixed_controls.candidate_builder_policy: current runtime prunes broad abstention options when concrete frequency candidates are present

decision.g10_routing: Do not use raw inventory labels alone as exact-label answer options. Current coverage is 0/21 exact, 14/21 Purist-equivalent, and 17/21 Pragmatic-equivalent; aggregated gold labels may need to be constructed downstream from separately reported event pieces.

candidate_records.evidence_text: Seizure history since the last review: In November he had a run of one seizure within half an hour (not full generalised tonic-clonic, fluctuating in intensity, resolved without medication). In December there was 7 further seizures at night, and in March another during physiotherapy (unwitnessed by mother). He associates the November and December events with multiple red-eye flights, jet lag and fragmented sleep. The March episode followed a strenuous rehabilitation session for a minor ankle injury while he was mildly sleep-deprived; there was no reported trauma, tongue biting or incontinence.

candidate_records.evidence_text:
Feb two generalised tonic-clonic seizures were reported. In Mar a nocturnal seizure occurred, and in Apr another during respite care. The daytime events were said to begin without a consistent warning; witnesses describe bilateral stiffening and rhythmic jerks lasting approximately 90–120 seconds with a bitten lateral tongue on one occasion and post-event confusion for 30–45 minutes. The nocturnal event was inferred from a bed partner report of witnessed jerking, vocalisation, and subsequent morning myalgia, tongue soreness, and headache. The episode during respite care was recorded in the facility log with similar semiology and a comparable period of post-event disorientation.

Between events he reports intermittent fatigue and reduced concentration but no clear focal neurological symptoms. He is currently taking levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily as his sole antiseizure medicine, which he started eight months ago; adherence is reported as good with a single missed evening dose in late March. He denies alcohol excess or recreational drug use. Sleep has been fragmented since February owing to an
candidate_records.derivation: named-month event tallies summed over listed months
